magic.h(3) Library Functions Manual magic.h(3)

NAME

magic.h - Magic number validation constants and macros.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define MAGIC_FRAME_VALID 0xA5C11C4A1ULL
Magic number for valid ring buffer frames (0xA5C11C4A1 = ASCIICHAT"inhex). #define MAGIC_FRAME_FREED 0xFEEDFACEU
Magic number for freed ring buffer frames (0xFEEDFACE). #define MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_VALID 0xBF00B001U
Magic number for valid pooled buffers (0xBF00B001). #define MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_FALLBACK 0xBF00FA11U
Magic number for malloc fallback buffers (0xBF00FA11). #define MAGIC_PACKET_VALID 0xA5C11C4A1ULL
Magic number for network packets (0xA5C11C4A1 = ASCIICHAT"inhex). #define IS_MAGIC_VALID(magic, expected)
Check if magic number is valid (true if matches expected). #define IS_FRAME_VALID(frame)
Check if frame is valid and not freed. #define IS_FRAME_FREED(frame)
Check if frame has been freed (corruption detection). #define IS_BUFFER_POOL_VALID(node)
Check if buffer pool node is valid. #define MARK_FRAME_FREED(frame)
Mark a frame as freed.

Detailed Description

Magic number validation constants and macros.

Definition in file magic.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define IS_BUFFER_POOL_VALID( node)

Value:

((node)->magic == MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_VALID || (node)->magic == MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_FALLBACK)




Check if buffer pool node is valid.


Definition at line 45 of file magic.h.


45 #define IS_BUFFER_POOL_VALID(node)                                                                                     \
46   ((node)->magic == MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_VALID || (node)->magic == MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_FALLBACK)










#define
  IS_FRAME_FREED( frame)


Value:


((frame)->magic == MAGIC_FRAME_FREED)






Check if frame has been freed (corruption detection).


Definition at line 42 of file magic.h.


Referenced by framebuffer_read_frame().






#define
  IS_FRAME_VALID( frame)


Value:


((frame)->magic == MAGIC_FRAME_VALID && (frame)->data != NULL)






Check if frame is valid and not freed.


Definition at line 39 of file magic.h.


Referenced by framebuffer_clear(), and
    framebuffer_write_frame().






#define
  IS_MAGIC_VALID( magic, expected)


Value:


((magic) == (expected))






Check if magic number is valid (true if matches expected).


Definition at line 36 of file magic.h.


Referenced by buffer_pool_free(),
    framebuffer_clear(), framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame(),
    framebuffer_read_frame(), framebuffer_read_multi_frame(), and
    framebuffer_write_frame().






#define
  MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_FALLBACK 0xBF00FA11U


Magic number for malloc fallback buffers (0xBF00FA11).


Definition at line 26 of file magic.h.


Referenced by buffer_pool_alloc(), and
    buffer_pool_free().






#define
  MAGIC_BUFFER_POOL_VALID 0xBF00B001U


Magic number for valid pooled buffers (0xBF00B001).


Definition at line 23 of file magic.h.


Referenced by buffer_pool_alloc(), and
    buffer_pool_free().






#define
  MAGIC_FRAME_FREED 0xFEEDFACEU


Magic number for freed ring buffer frames (0xFEEDFACE).


Definition at line 20 of file magic.h.






#define
  MAGIC_FRAME_VALID 0xA5C11C4A1ULL


Magic number for valid ring buffer frames (0xA5C11C4A1 =
    "ASCIICHAT" in hex).


Definition at line 17 of file magic.h.


Referenced by framebuffer_write_frame(), and
    framebuffer_write_multi_frame().






#define
  MAGIC_PACKET_VALID 0xA5C11C4A1ULL


Magic number for network packets (0xA5C11C4A1 =
    "ASCIICHAT" in hex).


Definition at line 29 of file magic.h.






#define
  MARK_FRAME_FREED( frame)


Value:


((frame)->magic = MAGIC_FRAME_FREED)






Mark a frame as freed.


Definition at line 49 of file magic.h.


Referenced by framebuffer_clear(), and
    framebuffer_write_frame().
